questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: According to the statutes of the Nobel Foundation regarding the division of the prize, which of the following scenarios is permissible?

options.text: A single prize amount is divided equally between four individual researchers for a single collaborative work.
A prize is awarded to two distinct works, with the amount divided equally between them.
The prize for Literature is awarded jointly to four authors who co-wrote a single book.
The prize money is divided between two works, one receiving two-thirds and the other receiving one-third of the total.

questions.explanation: According to the statutes, a prize amount may be equally divided between two works. If a work is produced by two or three persons, it can be awarded jointly. However, in no case may a prize be divided between more than three persons. Option B is specifically mentioned as a rule for dividing the amount between two works.
questions.takeaway: Nobel Prize statutes strictly limit the number of recipients to three persons per prize and allow for the division of the award between two distinct works.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: The statutes of the Nobel Foundation says that a prize amount may be equally divided between two works, each of which is considered to merit a prize... In no case may a prize amount be divided between more than three persons.
